title: Känguru der Mathematik 2015 — Felix (Pré-Écolier, Grades 1-2)
source_organization: Känguru der Mathematik (Austria) / Kangourou sans Frontières
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper (kaenguru.at, via matematica.pt). For personal educational practice only.
answer_source: Official 2015_Answers.pdf answer table (Felix row), read and verified.

questions.question_text: Which shape cannot be seen in every picture?
questions.solution: Check each shape across all four pictures. The green square is missing from at least one picture, so it is not in every one.
solution_steps: Take each shape and look for it in all four pictures.
The green square does not appear in every picture.
So the answer is the green square.

questions.question_text: How many triangles can you find in the picture (of the girl)?
questions.solution: Count every triangle in the girl drawing — the bow, collar, skirt and so on. There are 5 triangles.
solution_steps: Look over the whole drawing for triangle shapes.
Count each one (bow, collar, skirt pieces, etc.).
There are 5 triangles.

questions.question_text: Which part of the house is missing?
questions.solution: Look at the gap in the house outline and match its exact shape. Only piece E fits the missing space.
solution_steps: Find the empty gap in the house.
Match the shape of the gap to each piece.
Piece E fits exactly.

questions.question_text: How many dots do all the ladybirds have together?
questions.solution: Count the dots on each of the five ladybirds and add them up. The total is 19 dots.
solution_steps: Count the dots on each ladybird.
Add the five amounts together.
The total is 19.

questions.question_text: Florian bolts 10 equal metal strips together in pairs, overlapping some holes, making five long strips. Which of the long strips is the shortest?
questions.solution: The more holes two strips share when bolted, the more they overlap and the shorter the result. Strip B overlaps the most, so it is the shortest.
solution_steps: Overlapping more holes makes a shorter combined strip.
Compare how much each pair overlaps.
Strip B overlaps most, so it is shortest.

questions.question_text: Which of the kangaroo cards can be turned around so that it then looks the same as the card shown on the right?
questions.solution: Only rotating is allowed (no mirror flip). The card that becomes identical to the reference after a turn is card E.
solution_steps: You may only rotate the card, not flip it.
Test which card matches the reference after turning.
Card E matches.

questions.question_text: What do you see if you look at the tower (a cylinder with a cone on top) exactly from above?
questions.solution: Looking straight down, the cone's tip is in the centre and the round cylinder forms the outline, so you see a circle.
solution_steps: Look straight down onto the tower.
The round cylinder gives a circular outline.
You see a circle.

questions.question_text: How many numbers are outside the square?
questions.solution: The square holds 9, 24, 21 and 36. The numbers 52 and 48 lie in the circle but outside the square, so 2 numbers are outside the square.
solution_steps: List the numbers inside the square: 9, 24, 21, 36.
The rest are 52 and 48.
So 2 numbers are outside the square.

questions.question_text: It takes Jennifer half an hour to cover half of her journey home. How long does the whole journey take?
questions.solution: Half the journey takes half an hour, so the whole journey takes twice that: 2 × 30 minutes = 1 hour.
solution_steps: Half the trip = half an hour (30 min).
The whole trip is twice the half.
2 × 30 = 60 minutes = 1 hour.

questions.question_text: Michael has two building blocks, each made of two cubes glued together. Which figure can he NOT make using the blocks?
questions.solution: Each block is a straight 1×2 domino, so any figure must split into two straight dominoes. Figure B is a T-shape that cannot be split into two dominoes, so it cannot be made.
solution_steps: Each block is a straight pair of cubes (a domino).
A figure works only if it splits into two straight dominoes.
The T-shaped figure B cannot, so B.

questions.question_text: Julia has 9 sweets and Katharina has 17 sweets. How many must Katharina give Julia so they both have the same amount?
questions.solution: Together they have 9 + 17 = 26 sweets, so each should have 13. Katharina gives away 17 − 13 = 4 sweets.
solution_steps: Total sweets = 9 + 17 = 26, so each gets 13.
Katharina has 17 and needs 13.
She gives away 17 − 13 = 4.

questions.question_text: Each of six building blocks has 5 little cubes, white or grey, with equal colours never touching. How many little white cubes are there in total?
questions.solution: Because colours alternate and never touch, each block of 5 cubes has 2 white and 3 grey. Six blocks give 6 × 2 = 12 white cubes.
solution_steps: Colours alternate, so a 5-cube block has 2 white and 3 grey.
There are 6 such blocks.
White cubes = 6 × 2 = 12.

questions.question_text: Which triangular piece is missing from the star-patterned rhombus?
questions.solution: The star pattern and the way the triangle points must line up with its neighbours. The piece that completes the pattern in the gap is A.
solution_steps: Look at how the star pattern continues around the gap.
Match the pattern and the triangle's direction.
Piece A fits the gap.

questions.question_text: 11 flags are placed along a straight race course, the first at the start and the last at the finish, always 8 metres apart. How long is the race course?
questions.solution: Between 11 flags there are 11 − 1 = 10 gaps, each 8 metres, so the course is 10 × 8 = 80 metres.
solution_steps: 11 flags make 11 − 1 = 10 gaps between them.
Each gap is 8 metres.
Length = 10 × 8 = 80 metres.

questions.question_text: Some pirates climb onto a ship one after another. Their leader is exactly in the middle and is the eighth to climb on. How many pirates board the ship?
questions.solution: Being 8th and exactly in the middle means 7 pirates are before him and 7 after, so the total is 7 + 1 + 7 = 15.
solution_steps: The leader is 8th, so 7 pirates climbed before him.
Being in the middle, 7 pirates climb after him too.
